name: Tutoring & Training
description: Enquiry-to-enrolment pipeline for a tutoring centre or training provider, with Courses and Enrolments tracked as their own records.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a tutoring centre / training provider. A contact is normally the fee payer — a parent or guardian, an adult learner, or a corporate L&D buyer — and the student named on the record may be a different person. A deal is one enrolment decision for one student, running from first enquiry through a level assessment and trial lesson to signed enrolment and paid fees. Courses and enrolments are separate records; never invent a course code, timetable slot, tutor name, exam board or fee you were not given.

automation_rules.name: Chase a new enquiry into a trial lesson
automation_rules.description: Parents enquire at three centres on the same evening — whoever books the trial first usually gets the enrolment.

params.title: Call {{contact.first_name}} — confirm subject and level, then book a trial lesson
New tutoring enquiry
params.body: Enquiry received. Capture the student's name, year group, target exam or exam board, current grade, and the days and times they can attend before quoting a fee.

automation_rules.name: Keep a stage history on every enrolment
automation_rules.description: Writes a timeline note each time an enrolment deal moves, so whoever picks the family up next can see where they got to.

params.title: Enrolment stage updated: {{deal.title}}
params.body: Stage changed. Record what was agreed — the tutor assigned, the timetable slot, the fee quoted, and anything the family still has to decide.

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We are a tutoring centre serving [your town / catchment area]. Replace this with who you are, the year groups and exam boards you cover, and how long you have been teaching.

## Centre details

- Address and parking / drop-off arrangements
- Opening hours, including which evenings and weekend slots run
- Safeguarding and DBS policy in one line, plus who parents should ask for

## Term dates

List your term start and end dates and any weeks you close.
kb_templates.products:
## What we teach

- **One-to-one tuition** — [subjects], [fee] per hour
- **Small group classes** — max [n] students, [fee] per session
- **Exam preparation** — [GCSE / A-Level / IB / SAT], intensive courses in the [n] weeks before papers
- **Holiday intensives and catch-up courses**

## Fees and terms

State your session length, minimum commitment (per term / per block of [n] sessions), payment plans you accept, and your cancellation notice period.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying an enquiry

1. Who is the student — year group, school, current grade, and target grade.
2. Subject and exam board. Get the board right; a wrong-board tutor loses the family in week two.
3. What has already been tried, and what is not working.
4. Availability — which evenings and weekend slots actually work for them.
5. Budget and whether they want one-to-one or a group.

## Converting a trial

Call the parent within 24 hours of the trial. Lead with one specific observation about the student's work, name the gap you would close first, and propose a concrete timetable slot and start date.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to enrolment

1. **New Enquiry** — log the contact, the student's name and the subject they want.
2. **Needs & Level Assessment** — placement test or diagnostic call; confirm level and exam board.
3. **Trial Lesson Booked** — a named tutor, a date and a time in the diary.
4. **Trial Attended** — tutor feedback captured while it is fresh.
5. **Fee Quote Sent** — sessions per week, fee, payment plan and start date in writing.
6. **Enrolled** — enrolment form signed and first payment received; create the Enrolment record against the Course.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Why families choose us

Replace with your genuine differentiators. The ones that actually decide it in tutoring are: tutors who know the specific exam board, small and stable group sizes, measured progress the parent can see, and the same tutor every week rather than a rota.

## Common comparisons

- **Online marketplaces** — cheaper per hour, but no continuity and no one accountable for the grade.
- **Big chain centres** — recognisable brand, larger classes, less flexibility on timetable.
- **Private freelance tutors** — flexible and personal, but no cover when the tutor is ill and no progress reporting.
